methodology: WebSearch + WebFetch across Equinor crude assay page, norskpetroleum.no, sodir.no, OGJ Guide to World Crudes, SPE/AAPG papers, offshore-technology.com, NS Energy, Wikipedia, ExxonMobil crude assays. API gravity values prefer the Equinor commercial blend assay (proxy for reservoir crude); reservoir-level data from norskpetroleum.no / Sodir / SPE papers.

fields.formation: Brent Group (Middle Jurassic) + Statfjord Group / Cook Fm (Lower Jurassic / Upper Triassic)
fields.notes: Light, low-sulfur crude (~0.22 wt% S). Reservoirs at 2500-3000 m in tilted fault block. Discovered 1974, on stream 1979. Operator Equinor since 1987. Statfjord Blend is a commingled stream (Statfjord + Snorre + Sygna + Statfjord N/E).

fields.formation: Brent Group (Middle Jurassic) dominant; Cook Fm + Statfjord Fm (Lower Jurassic)
fields.notes: In-situ reservoir API ~32-36 (Brent), blend is 39.3 due to lighter Cook/Statfjord contribution and condensate stripping. Initial GOR reported 100 ft3/ft3 (~18 Sm3/Sm3). ~80% of reserves in Brent. Overpressured. Datum 1850 m bMSL.

fields.formation: Brent Group (Middle Jurassic): Oseberg, Rannoch, Etive, Ness, Tarbert Fms
fields.notes: Reservoir at 2300-2700 m (locally to 3100). T/P at 2497 m MSL is 99.8 C, 280.7 bar. HC column 600 m (380 m gas + 210 m oil). Porosity ~24%, perm 1-4 D. Sulfur ~0.27 wt%. Oseberg Blend feeds the Brent-Oseberg basket.

fields.formation: Sognefjord Fm (Late Jurassic) + Fensfjord Fm (Middle Jurassic) - shallow marine sandstone
fields.notes: Troll Ost: gas cap up to 250 m + thin oil rim 0-9 m. Troll Vest Gas: 210 m gas + 12 m oil. Troll Vest Oil: 43 m gas + 28 m oil. Porosity up to 35%, perm in darcy range. Oil produced via long horizontal wells; gas by pressure depletion. Reservoir depths Troll Ost 1330 m, Troll Vest 1360 m.

fields.formation: Intra-Draupne Fm sandstone (Upper Jurassic); secondary in Statfjord Gp, Vestland Gp, Viking Gp spiculites, Zechstein carbonates
fields.notes: Oil density 0.89 g/cc (~882.8 kg/m3 at 15 C). Hydrostatic pressure, undersaturated oil with low GOR (~40 Sm3/Sm3). Sulfur 0.8 wt% (medium sour). Porosity 28%, perm 0.5-40 Darcy. Net/gross 97%. Recovery target >70% (one of highest in NCS). Discovery 2010, on stream 2019.

fields.api_source: Comingled into Statfjord Blend (Equinor assay) - proxy
fields.formation: Lunde Fm (Late Triassic) + Statfjord Gp (Lower Jurassic); minor Alke Fm
fields.notes: Reservoir 2000-2700 m. ~75% of OOIP in Lunde Fm. Undersaturated oil. GOR increases SW to NE across field. Perm 2000 mD upper Statfjord Fm, 125-380 mD below. Production blended into Statfjord stream.

fields.formation: Are Fm + Tilje Fm + Ile Fm + Garn Fm (Lower-Middle Jurassic, Fangst Gp)
fields.notes: Heavy, medium-sulfur crude. Highly faulted/segmented. Lower Jurassic Are + Tilje OOIP ~2160 mmbbl (~343 Msm3). Gas cap above oil. Mid-Norway shelf, water depth 350 m. World's first concrete TLP. Recovery via gas inj (Garn/Ile) + water inj (Are/Tilje).

fields.formation: Garn, Ile, Tofte, Tilje, Are Fms (Jurassic)
fields.notes: Extra light, low-sulfur condensate blend (also incl. Kristin and Mikkel). Reservoir to 4850 m. Smorbukk, Smorbukk Sor, Midgard segments. Reserves: 830 mmbbl oil/condensate + 212 bcm gas. Garn Fm best quality. Halten Banken, Norwegian Sea.

fields.formation: Ile + Tofte Fms (oil); Not Fm (gas) - Lower-Middle Jurassic, Bat/Fangst Gps
fields.notes: Light, low-sulfur sweet crude. Porosity 25-30%, perm 20-2500 mD. HC column to 135 m (110 m oil + 25 m gas). Reservoir 2500-2700 m. Water injection drive; expected ultimate recovery >60% (highest of subsea oil reservoirs). FPSO at 380 m water depth. Cape Vulture discovery (2018) doubled remaining reserves.

fields.api_source: Inferred from Heimdal-Fm analog (Grane heavy oil region) - similar to Grane heavy crude
fields.formation: Heimdal Fm (Paleocene) - marine turbidite sandstones
fields.notes: Reserves ~200 mmbbl (~30 MSm3). Subsea tie-back to Grane platform. Same Heimdal Fm reservoir as Grane (which is 18.7 API) - suggests heavy oil, likely 20-25 API. Production from 22 wells. PDO 2021, first oil Oct 2023. Pressure depletion + gas lift. Field located NE of Grane.

fields.api_source: Typical Hugin Fm condensate range; not in Equinor commercial assay list
fields.formation: Hugin Fm (Middle Jurassic) sandstone
fields.notes: Gas/condensate field (oil rim under gas cap). Reservoir 3300-3900 m, 14 fault segments. Initially produced by gas injection, since 2021 by pressure depletion. Test rates 225 km3/d gas + 120 m3/d condensate. Came on stream June 2017. Tied to Sleipner A for processing.

fields.formation: Draupne Fm (Upper Jurassic, main) + Hugin Fm (Middle Jurassic)
fields.notes: Extra light, low-sulfur HPHT condensate. Reservoir 4000-4700 m. Initial reservoir pressure ~820 bar. Draupne Fm hosts main recoverable resources (good quality); Hugin variable. Field reserves 184 mmboe. Tied to Sleipner A via two pipelines (oil + wet gas). 55 km N of Sleipner.

fields.api_source: Condensate typical (50-70 API range stated by source); not in Equinor commercial assay list
fields.formation: Brent Group (Middle Jurassic); secondary Cook Fm + Statfjord Gp
fields.notes: HPHT gas/condensate field. Initial pore pressure 775 bar, fracture 875 bar, T 155-163 C. Two-stage GOR 1100 Sm3/Sm3, dewpoint 452 bara at 163 C. 8.6 mol% C7+. Block 34/11, Tampen Spur. Rich gas to Kollsnes, condensate to Mongstad.

fields.api_source: Comingled into Gullfaks/Statfjord-area stream; using NCS Brent-area light proxy
fields.formation: Lunde Fm + Statfjord Gp (Late Triassic-Early Jurassic) + Brent Gp (Middle Jurassic)
fields.notes: Two reservoir groups: Statfjord/Amundsen and Brent. Multiple tilted fault blocks. P 425-450 bar, T 115 C. GOR 180-230 Sm3/Sm3 (some segments to 266/512). Mainly pressure depletion + partial water inj. PDO 1996, on stream Apr 1999. Tampen area.

fields.api_source: Typical HPHT condensate range; not in Equinor commercial assay list
fields.formation: Brent Group (Middle Jurassic) + Cook Fm (Lower Jurassic)
fields.notes: HPHT gas/condensate. Reservoir 3900-4200 m. Recovery ~18 bcm gas + 192 mmboe condensate. 160 km W of Bergen. Drilled longest HPHT well to date by Statoil (7380 m).

fields.api_source: Likely commingled into Troll Blend stream (Troll C platform), proxy ~Troll
fields.formation: Brent Group (Middle Jurassic) + Draupne Fm (Upper Jurassic marine fan) + Sognefjord Fm (Upper Jurassic shallow marine)
fields.notes: Originally 66.3 Msm3 o.e. recoverable (43.7 Msm3 oil + 19.5 Msm3 gas + 3.1 Msm3 NGL). Reservoirs 2300-2500 m. Tied to Troll C; oil to Mongstad via TOP-II; gas via Troll A to Kollsnes. Fram Ost: aquifer drive in Brent + Sognefjord. Fram Vest: pressure depletion.

fields.formation: Heimdal Fm (Paleocene) - marine turbidite sandstone
fields.notes: Heavy, medium-sulfur crude. In-situ reservoir oil reported at 18.5-18.7 API by NSEnergy/Wiki sources (very heavy, viscosity 12 cp); commercial blend stream is 27.1 API after blending with other Heimdal feeds. Pay 50 m avg, porosity 33%, perm 5-10 D. Norway's first heavy oil field. Gas inj top + horizontal wells bottom + limited water inj.

fields.formation: Garn, Ile, Tofte Fms (Middle Jurassic)
fields.notes: Deeply buried HPHT condensate field. Reservoirs ~4500 m (Garn) and 4850 m (Ile). Ile Fm conditions 169 C / 911 bar. Reserves 42 bcm gas + 35 Msm3 condensate. Sub-Arctic, Halten Terrace. Condensate commingled into Aasgard Blend.

fields.api_source: Inferred Halten Banken light oil/condensate; not in Equinor commercial assay list
fields.formation: Garn Fm (Middle Jurassic) main; Ile Fm secondary
fields.notes: Oil + gas/condensate fields (Tyrihans N + Tyrihans S). Garn Fm main reservoir at 3500 m. Tied to Kristin platform. Recent 2025 discovery (6407/1-B-2 H) found 63 m gas/condensate in Ile + 6 m condensate/light oil in Garn.

fields.formation: Garn, Ile, Tofte Fms (Middle Jurassic)
fields.notes: Gas/condensate field with thin underlying oil zone. 300 m HC column. Six fault-segmented structures, all with good reservoir quality. Reserves 28 bcm gas + 40 mmbbl condensate. Pressure depletion. On stream 2003, tied to Aasgard B. Condensate commingled into Aasgard Blend.
